tagline: Chicago-based trucking carrier for owner operators and CDL-A drivers
primaryAudience: Owner operators and CDL-A company drivers (Chicago / Midwest focus)
secondaryAudience: Fleet operators considering a switch; shippers needing freight services

voice.tone: Direct, confident, no-fluff. Talks to drivers like a knowledgeable peer who has run the numbers, not a corporate marketer.
voice.personality: Honest about trade-offs. Names specific dollar amounts and percentages, never vague ranges. Counters generic advice with evidence.
voice.perspective: Written from an insider's seat — dispatcher / carrier ops / experienced operator. Acknowledges driver frustrations openly.

doSay: Specific dollar amounts, miles, percentages, dates ($240K-$290K, 6.5 MPG, 95,000 miles)
Empathy openers: 'If you've been searching X, you've probably noticed Y'
Bold lead-ins on numbered insights: **1. They run higher-paying freight, not more miles.**
Bullet lists with $$$ ranges or hard data per line
Internal links woven naturally into sentences (not a 'see also' dump)
Subtle carrier framing — 'the right carrier' / 'a carrier built around drivers, not against them'
Honest trade-offs (maintenance risk, slow weeks, transmission failures)
Em-dashes for emphasis — they fit this voice
dontSay: Welcome to our blog / Welcome back
Buckle up, let's dive in, let's explore
It's important to note / It goes without saying / In conclusion
Click-baity questions ('Did you know...?', 'What if I told you...?')
AI-tells like 'In the realm of', 'When it comes to', 'At the end of the day'

internalLinks./: XXII Century home / Owner Operators landing

examples: why drivers leave in first 90 days
career path from company driver to owner op

structureRules.headings: H1 title + 4-6 H2 sections + optional H3 subsections
structureRules.openingPattern: Hook addressing the reader's frustration or specific question — never a generic intro
structureRules.ctaPlacement: End with a CTA mentioning phone + apply URL
structureRules.images: 1 hero (Fal-generated) at the top; optional 1-2 in-body images via ![alt](url)
structureRules.alsoReadCallout: Optional `_Also Read: [title](/blog/slug)` line linking one existing post

_note: Read by the n8n blog-automation workflow before drafting. Update as the brand voice or service lineup evolves. Existing-post slugs prevent the LLM from re-pitching topics that already exist.
